channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump claims a win on immigration after a call with Mexico's president. But she suggests no change
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is declaring a win on stopping illegal immigration through Mexico after talking with that country’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Feds can’t destroy razor wire Texas installed near Eagle Pass, appeals court rules
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton had sued the Biden administration after Border Patrol agents had cut the razor wire.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandro Serrano, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's transition says several of his Cabinet picks and appointees have been targeted by bomb threats and “swatting attacks.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House pressing Ukraine to draft 18-year-olds so it has enough troops to battle Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration is urging Ukraine to quickly increase the size of its military by drafting more troops and revamping its mobilization laws to allow for the conscription of those as young as 18.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mississippi runoff election for state Supreme Court justice is too close to call
item.description: A runoff election in the state Supreme Court in Mississippi race is too close to call, with State Sen. Jenifer Branning and Justice Jim Kitchens neck and neck as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats in Pennsylvania had a horrible 2024 election. They say it's still a swing state
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The drubbing Democrats took in Pennsylvania in this year's election has prompted predictable vows to rebound, but it has also sowed doubts about whether Pennsylvania might be leaving the ranks of up-for-grabs swing states for a right-leaning existence more like Ohio's.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump turns to critic of COVID mandates to run NIH
item.description: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, a Stanford health researcher, is in line to lead the National Institutes of Health. Early in the pandemic he argued against lockdowns and focusing on people at highest risk.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, GARANCE BURKE and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz, agriculture leaders celebrate Minnesota turkey production, show concern over tariffs
item.description: DFL Gov. Tim Walz continued the tradition of celebrating Minnesota’s nation-leading position when it comes to turkey production. He also addressed preliminary concerns of tariffs promised by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court agrees to end Trump’s classified documents case
item.description: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals on Tuesday granted a request from special counsel Jack Smith to drop President-elect Trump’s Florida documents case, ending the battle to charge him over retaining records with classified markings after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida health official advises communities to stop adding fluoride to drinking water
item.description: Florida’s surgeon general, Dr. Joseph Ladapo, cited developmental concerns from higher levels of fluoride than are found in most U.S. water supplies.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Colombini, MPR News

item.title: Battleground Florida: Economist explains potential impact of Trump's tariffs
item.description: On WFLA's Sunday morning political show Battleground Florida, University of Tampa Economics Professor Abiail Hall Blanco explained the purpose of tariffs and how they impact families and businesses in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s deportation vow alarms Texas construction industry
item.description: Texas builders warn mass deportations of undocumented migrants could devastate the construction industry, threatening housing and infrastructure work in one of the nation’s fastest-growing states.
item.dc:creator: Julian Aguilar, MPR News

item.title: Florida officials allege 'pervasive' discrimination against Trump supporters at FEMA
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody and U.S. Rep. Vern Buchanan will hold a news conference Friday "to discuss their efforts to uncover evidence of political discrimination at FEMA and demand accountability," according to a release from Buchanan's office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House passes bill that would allow Treasury to target nonprofits it deems to support terrorism
item.description: The U.S. House passed legislation that would give the Treasury Department unilateral authority to strip the tax-exempt status of nonprofits it claims support terrorism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here’s how Trump and the GOP may try to weaken Obamacare
item.description: Republican control of the White House and Congress sets the stage for potentially seismic changes including curtailing Medicaid, weakening patient protections, and increasing premium costs for millions.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Armour, Sam Whitehead, and Julie Rovner, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11
